_run.note: 1 researcher + one independent skeptic per finding. 6 confirmed, 1 refuted — the verify pass demoted a finding the researcher had asserted.

confirmed: fanout-execute calls assertDisjoint() (flows/lib/ownership.js) in its Gate phase before any dispatch; overlapping exclusive_write sets throw. [fanout-execute.flow.js:29-32, ownership.js:65-76]
flows/lib/schemas.js is the single source of truth; flows import its objects for runtime agent({schema}) validation. [schemas.js:1-15,140-145]
schemas/*.json are generated from schemas.js by tools/gen-schemas.mjs; tools/check.sh is the drift guard. [gen-schemas.mjs:13-24, check.sh]
research-verify fans out one skeptic per key_finding that re-opens cited files, defaults holds=false, and only holds=true findings land in `confirmed`. [research-verify.flow.js:34-83]

refuted.claim: check.sh only checks that each generated schema EXISTS, not that it matches committed — so it misses byte-level drift.
refuted.reason: Refuted by re-reading the file: check.sh runs `git diff --quiet -- schemas/` after regeneration and fails on any byte difference. The verifier cited the exact lines, demoting a claim that was stale relative to the current source.
refuted.demonstrates: The verify pass gates output against current evidence rather than trusting the researcher's single read.
